{"id":"smallhold-iwry","title":"Delivery queue: collect inboxes, dedup by sharedInbox, insert rows","description":"When a post is created, collect all target inboxes: for public/unlisted posts, all follower inboxes; for private, followers only; for direct, mentioned actor inboxes. Dedup by shared_inbox_url (if a remote server has shared inbox, use it once, not per-follower). Insert one delivery_queue row per unique inbox URL.

Agent workflow: Spawn a research subagent to enumerate the follower inbox collection query and verify it handles the case where some followers have shared inboxes and some don't.

Test discipline:
- Post with 3 followers on mastodon.social (shared inbox) → 1 delivery row, not 3
- Post with 2 followers on different servers → 2 delivery rows
- Direct post to 2 mentioned actors on same server → 1 delivery row (shared inbox)
- Empty follower list → 0 delivery rows

Defensive programming:
- Do not enqueue delivery to localhost or private IP ranges (SSRF prevention)
- next_attempt_at = now for new rows (deliver immediately)","acceptance_criteria":"3 followers on same server → 1 delivery row; followers on different servers → separate rows; SSRF targets rejected; next_attempt_at is 'now'","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:35:36Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:35:36Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-70g","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:35:41Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
